sectionsUnavailable: Authenticated marketplace detail-page extraction beyond the public sample PDF is not available yet.
No medication list, medication-specific question, or calibrated drug-response model is bundled with this synthetic fixture.
Sample rows show observed public sample-report structure; user-specific medication response requires local pharmacogene evidence plus clinical context.

resultRows.item: Public Medication & Drug Response sample table
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The public sample PDF shows a result table with trait, result, and description columns plus a drug-response summary section.
resultRows.description: Sample-table structure is used to keep the local agent output aligned with the formal report shape; it is not a user-specific medication result.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This tells the agent what fields the report should contain, while local genomeEvidence determines any user-specific PGx rows.

resultRows.description: Abacavir is a medication used to prevent and treat HIV/AIDS.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This is an observed sample-report row for Abacavir Hypersensitivity; it preserves output structure and is not a user-specific medication recommendation.

resultRows.description: A chronic disease characterised by uncontrolled drinking and preoccupation with alcohol.

resultRows.description: Cannabis, also known as marijuana, is a psychoactive drug.

resultRows.description: Cocaine is a highly addictive drug associated with health issues and other problems.

resultRows.description: Hepatitis C is a virus that causes liver damage and is often spread through the sharing of unclean needles.

resultRows.description: Nicotine is a highly addictive stimulant most commonly acquired through smoking cigarettes.

resultRows.description: Warfarin is a medication used as an anticoagulant; the sample notes that slower metabolism requires lower dose.

resultRows.description: Warfarin is a medication used as an anticoagulant; the sample notes that elevated sensitivity requires lower dose.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This is an observed sample-report row for Warfarin Sensitivity; it preserves output structure and is not a user-specific medication recommendation.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived CYP2C19 call is available for medication-response context. It is not a medication recommendation.
resultRows.description: CYP2C19 is one of the pharmacogenes commonly represented in medication-response workflows.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This row can help organize a clinician or pharmacist discussion, but it should not be used alone to change medication.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived CYP2D6 call is available, but CYP2D6 can require specialized calling and confirmation.
resultRows.description: CYP2D6 interpretation can depend on allele definitions and copy-number handling that are not fully represented in this synthetic fixture.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Treat this as derived context only; medication decisions need professional review and appropriate confirmatory evidence.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived SLCO1B1 rs4149056 TC call is available for statin-response context.
resultRows.description: This marker is included as local PGx context, not as a standalone safety or dosing instruction.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The result is relevant to medication-response discussions, but it is not a standalone prescribing instruction.

clinicianReview: Share PGx findings with a clinician or pharmacist before making medication decisions.
Keep current prescriptions unchanged unless a licensed professional advises otherwise.
Ask whether confirmatory clinical testing is appropriate for a specific medication question.
Use the public sample PDF only as output-structure guidance, not as your personal medication-response result.

references.title: FDA Table of Pharmacogenomic Biomarkers in Drug Labeling

missingInputs: Authenticated marketplace detail-page sections beyond the public sample PDF have not been extracted.
CYP2C9/VKORC1 sample-summary rows are public sample structure unless local user-specific evidence supplies those calls.
HLA, CYP2D6 copy-number, MT-RNR1, DPYD, TPMT/NUDT15, and other specialized calls are not included in this fixture.
No medication list, current dose, age, clinical history, or clinician-reviewed medication question is supplied.
uncertainty: No calibrated medication-response probability model is supplied for this package.
PGx context depends on medication, dose, ancestry, health status, interacting medicines, and clinical history.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.
The report is educational and does not provide prescribing, dosing, start, stop, or switch instructions.
At-home or derived data should not be used alone for treatment decisions.
The public sample PDF demonstrates output shape and example rows, not this user-specific medication efficacy or safety.
